description: Sensory overload at its finest — a megacity of golden temples and rooftop bars, where a Michelin-starred meal and a street pad thai cost the same, and the energy never drops below electrifying.

neighborhoodRecs: Sukhumvit (Asok/Phrom Phong) — Modern, expat-friendly, best international restaurants, connected to BTS Skytrain.
Silom / Sathorn — Business district by day, rooftop bars and LGBT nightlife by night, easy transport links.
Banglamphu / Khao San Road — Backpacker central, cheap guesthouses, within walking distance of the Grand Palace.
Ari — Local neighbourhood, boutique cafes, fewer tourists, young Bangkok creative scene, great food.
areasToAvoid: Accepting rides from tuk-tuk drivers near Wat Pho who offer tours — they take you to gem shops and tailors for commission.
Patpong night market wallets — pickpocket teams work the narrow lanes, keep bags in front.
Flooding streets in September — some central areas flood during heavy monsoon rains.
scamWarnings: Grand Palace 'closed today' scam: friendly man near the palace says it's closed for a ceremony and offers to take you on a tuk-tuk tour instead. The palace is almost never fully closed. Walk past and verify.
Gem shop scam: tuk-tuk takes you to a 'government gem sale' where you're pressured to buy overpriced stones to 'resell at a profit at home.' There is no profit.
Taxi meter refusal: some cabs refuse to use the meter and quote fixed prices 3–5x the meter rate. Simply get out and find another cab.
Jet ski damage scam in coastal areas: jet ski operators claim you damaged the ski and demand thousands of baht. Pay only for documented pre-existing damage.

airportTransfer.cheapest: Airport Rail Link (Suvarnabhumi) to Phaya Thai — ฿45, takes 30 min, connects to BTS Skytrain. Budget travellers' choice.
airportTransfer.fastest: Taxi from Suvarnabhumi — ฿250–400 plus expressway tolls (฿25–75), takes 30–50 min. Use the official taxi queue.
airportTransfer.recommended: Airport Rail Link for central/Sukhumvit stay. Taxi for Silom, Banglamphu, or late night (rail link closes 12am).
airportTransfer.taxiEstimate: Metered taxi from Suvarnabhumi: ฿250–400 + ฿50 airport surcharge + expressway tolls. To Khao San Road expect ฿350–500 total.

tippingEtiquette: Tipping is not traditional Thai culture but is now expected in tourist contexts. ฿20–50 at casual restaurants, 10% at nicer places.
Massage tip: ฿50–100 for a traditional Thai massage, ฿100–200 for oil massage.
Wai greeting: press palms together and bow slightly. Return a wai when someone gives you one, unless they are significantly younger.
Never touch anyone's head or point feet at people or religious objects — considered deeply disrespectful.
Dress modestly at temples — shoulders and knees covered. Many provide sarongs at the entrance.

dailyCostBreakdown.accommodation: Hostel dorm ฿200–400 | Guesthouse ฿500–900 | Mid-range hotel ฿1500–3000 | Luxury ฿5000+
dailyCostBreakdown.meals: Street pad thai ฿50–80 | Som tam + sticky rice ฿80 | Restaurant dinner ฿200–600 | Rooftop bar meal ฿800+
dailyCostBreakdown.transport: BTS/MRT single ฿16–59 | Tuk-tuk ฿100–200 | Metered taxi ฿60–150 | Grab app (similar to Uber)
dailyCostBreakdown.activities: Grand Palace ฿500 | Wat Pho ฿200 | Thai massage (1hr) ฿250–400 | Floating market tour ฿800–1500

packingTips: Light, breathable fabrics only — Bangkok is 30–35°C year-round with high humidity.
Temple-appropriate clothing — lightweight long trousers or a scarf to wrap around hips.
Anti-humidity hair products — the humidity is relentless.
Comfortable sandals — you'll be removing shoes at temples constantly.
premium.bookingStrategy: November to February is peak season with the best weather. Book accommodation 2–3 months ahead for December. Songkran (Thai New Year water festival, mid-April) is chaotic and fun — or stay away entirely if you dislike getting drenched.
localTips: The Chao Phraya river ferry (฿15 per trip) is the fastest way to see the waterfront temples and escape the gridlock traffic.
Chatuchak Weekend Market (Saturday/Sunday): 15,000 stalls, get there before 11am before the heat becomes unbearable.
Or Tor Kor market near Chatuchak has the best quality fresh produce and prepared foods in Bangkok — try the mango sticky rice.
BTS Skytrain is the only way to move efficiently during rush hour. The street grid locks completely 7–9am and 5–7pm.
7-Eleven and FamilyMart sell genuinely good sandwiches, fruit, and drinks — useful for cheap breakfasts.
premium.bestTimeToVisit: November to February: 27–30°C, dry, low humidity (relatively), perfect weather for temple-hopping.
dayTrips: Ayutthaya — Ancient capital 80 min by train, UNESCO ruins of temple complexes, best hired by bicycle.
Damnoen Saduak Floating Market — 90 min by minibus, colorful floating vendors, most photogenic 7–9am.
Kanchanaburi — 2.5 hours by train, Bridge over the River Kwai, WWII history, waterfalls and jungle.
visaInfo: Most Western nationalities (US, UK, EU, Australia, Canada) receive a visa exemption on arrival — 60 days for many passports as of 2024 policy update; check Thailand's Ministry of Foreign Affairs for your specific nationality
Thai Tourist Visa (TR) available in advance from Thai embassies for nationalities that require it — 60 days single entry, extendable by 30 days at immigration offices
Visa on arrival available for select nationalities not covered by exemption — 15 days, 2,000 THB fee, queue at the VOA counter at Suvarnabhumi airport
Extension of stay: visit any provincial immigration office or the main Bangkok Immigration Bureau — 30-day extensions cost 1,900 THB
Overstaying your visa carries a 500 THB per day fine (up to 20,000 THB cap) payable on departure — do not overstay
currencyTips: Currency: Thai Baht (฿ / THB). Widely accepted; cards work at most hotels, malls, and larger restaurants but street food and markets are cash-only
Best exchange rates: SuperRich Thailand and Vasu Exchange offices in Bangkok consistently offer the best rates — avoid airport booths which are 5–10% worse
ATMs: widely available but charge a 220–250 THB foreign withdrawal fee per transaction — withdraw larger amounts to minimise fee impact
Avoid dynamic currency conversion (DCC) at ATMs and terminals — always choose THB to avoid unfavourable conversion rates
Kasikorn Bank and Bangkok Bank ATMs tend to have the fewest issues with foreign cards; always notify your home bank before travelling
premium.simCard: AIS and True Move H offer the best tourist SIMs — available at counters in all terminals at Suvarnabhumi (BKK) and Don Mueang (DMK) airports. A 30-day SIM with 30–100 GB data costs 300–600 THB. AIS has the widest 4G coverage nationwide. For eSIM, Airalo offers Thailand packages from around $10 USD for 15 days.
